title: Stenophyllus tenuifolius
intro: Stenophyllus tenuifolius, commonly known as Guaco or Mexican pennywort, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central America. The health benefits of Guaco include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Guaco include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Guaco include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and soothe skin irritations.
